chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Cr. Misc. Application No.198 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3219/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3220/2026 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of main case ------------------------- 25.02.2026 Mr. Wazeer Hussain Khoso, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Applicant Bashir Ahmed son of Muhammad Soomar is brother of deceased Gul Baz, who was murdered on 22.07.2024 and FIR of the incident was registered on 24.07.2024 by Imtiaz Khushk son of Sohbat Khan Khushk at P.S. Makli, District Thatta for offence under Sections 302, 324, 506/2, 337-H(ii), 114, 337-D, 34, PPC. Perusal of FIR reveals that deceased Gul Baz came at the scene of offence along with co-accused and they fired at complainant party, resultantly, Fida Hussain, Zia, brother of complainant Imtiaz and brother of applicant Gul Baz sustained firearm injuries, who later on succumbed to their injuries. After usual investigation IO submitted final report under section 173, Cr.PC whereby cognizance was taken by learned concerned Magistrate and case was sent up for trial to Sessions Court. After lapse of about one year of the alleged incident, applicant Bashir Ahmed, who is brother of deceased Gul Baz approached to DIGP Hydeabad and filed application against the complainant of this case Imtiaz Khushk and alleged that he is involved in the murder of his brother. On such application, DIGP Hyderabad vide order dated 02.10.2025 (available at Page 49) constituted the Board, comprising under the Chairmanship of SSP Badin, SSP District Thatta and ADIG Establishment, Hyderabad Range.

After lapse of about one year of the alleged incident, applicant Bashir Ahmed, who is brother of deceased Gul Baz approached to DIGP Hydeabad and filed application against the complainant of this case Imtiaz Khushk and alleged that he is involved in the murder of his brother. On such application, DIGP Hyderabad vide order dated 02.10.2025 (available at Page 49) constituted the Board, comprising under the Chairmanship of SSP Badin, SSP District Thatta and ADIG Establishment, Hyderabad Range. The Board recommended this case for further investigation (available at Page 51), holding that matter be reinvestigated not by District Police Thatta and on the recommendations of the Board, DIGP Hyderabad directed the SSP Matiari to supervise the reinvestigation and matter was assigned to PI Ahmed Yar Bajkani, SHO P.S. Banoth, District Matiari. PI Ahmed Yar Bajkani approached the learned trial Court for permission to reinvestigate the case/further investigation and such permission was accorded by learned trial Court vide order dated 08.12.2025. He further submits that all of sudden reinvestigation was called off by Matiari Police, without assigning any cogent reason, therefore, applicant approached to the Ex-Officio Justice of Peace and submitted application for direction to complete further investigation and report and on such application DIGP Hyderabad filed comments (available at Page 87) wherein he has given reason about calling off reinvestigation process on the ground that complainant of this case Imtiaz Ali Khushk approached DIGP Hyderabad and submitted application that there is no need of reinvestigation/further investigation in the matter and on his application reinvestigation/further investigation was called off. However, learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace refused to issue direction for reinvestigation/further investigation vide order dated 14.02.2026. Learned counsel for the applicant has pointed out that SSP Thatta has filed misstatement before learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace wherein he has taken stance that SSP Matiari conducted the reinvestigation / further investigation and submitted report before the concerned Court.

However, learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace refused to issue direction for reinvestigation/further investigation vide order dated 14.02.2026. Learned counsel for the applicant has pointed out that SSP Thatta has filed misstatement before learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace wherein he has taken stance that SSP Matiari conducted the reinvestigation / further investigation and submitted report before the concerned Court. He further submits that no such report has been submitted by SSP Matiari, which is also evident in report furnished by SSP Matiari in para 5 of his statement (available at Page 105). Learned counsel for applicant submits that it is settled law that door of further investigation cannot be closed just to dig out the actual truth, which is the basic right of the aggrieved family whose brother was killed in a suspicious condition and the version of the applicant party for killing his brother has not been brought on record, which is fundamental right of the applicant party. Contentions raised require consideration. Issue notice to the respondents as well as Prosecutor General Sindh for 25.03.2025. Till then, operation of impugned order shall remain suspended. However, DIGP Hyderabad is directed to send file of further investigation to SSP Matiari and SSP Matiari is directed to complete the process of further investigation and submit such report before the trial Court as well as before this Court on or before the next date of hearing. Office is directed to communicate this order to SSP Matiari and DIGP Hyderabad and learned trial Court, by fax and other modes, for compliance. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
